days.title: С кем? — Going places with people

vocab.exampleEn: I am going with a friend.
I am walking with a friend.
I am playing with my sister.
I am talking with my neighbour.

grammar.title: The instrumental case with с ("with someone")
grammar.explanation: To say you do something **with a person**, use **с + the instrumental case**. For most masculine nouns add **-ом** (друг → с друг**ом**). For feminine nouns ending in **-а/-я**, change to **-ой/-ей** (сестра → с сестр**ой**, подруга → с подруг**ой**). Note the stress and the spelling change: сестра → с сестр**о́й**.

examples.russian: Я иду в кино с другом.
examples.english: I am going to the cinema with a friend.
She lives with her sister.
We are walking together with the neighbour.

reading.title: С кем ты идёшь?
reading.text: — С кем ты идёшь в кино? — Я иду с братом и подругой. Мы любим кино.
reading.translation: — Who are you going to the cinema with? — I am going with my brother and a friend. We love cinema.

days.speakingPrompt: Say out loud who you do three everyday things with. For example: "Я гуляю с другом", "Я живу с сестрой", "Я работаю с соседом".

days.title: С мамой и папой — Family in the instrumental

vocab.exampleEn: I am talking with my dad.
He is walking with his wife.
I am playing with the child.

grammar.title: Instrumental endings after с (single people)
grammar.explanation: After hissing/soft consonants masculine nouns take **-ем** instead of -ом: муж → с муж**ем**. Feminine **-а** becomes **-ой** (мама → с мам**ой**), and **-я** becomes **-ёй/-ей** (жена → с жен**ой**, семья → с семь**ёй**). Plural "parents" uses **-ами/-ями**: родители → с родител**ями**. We will study the full plural pattern soon — for now just learn these family forms.

examples.russian: Я живу с мамой и папой.
examples.english: I live with my mum and dad.

examples.russian: Он гуляет с женой и ребёнком.
examples.english: He is walking with his wife and child.
We are relaxing with the family.

reading.title: Дома
reading.text: Я живу с мамой, папой и братом. Сестра живёт с мужем. Мы любим отдыхать вместе.
reading.translation: I live with my mum, dad and brother. My sister lives with her husband. We love to relax together.

days.speakingPrompt: Describe who lives in your home using "Я живу с...". Then say one person your relatives live with, e.g. "Сестра живёт с мужем".

days.title: Чай с молоком — "with" for food and drinks

vocab.exampleEn: Bread with cheese is very tasty.
I am eating bread with cheese.

grammar.title: с + instrumental for ingredients
grammar.explanation: The same **с + instrumental** also means "with" for food and drinks. Neuter nouns ending in **-о** take **-ом**: молоко → с молок**ом**. Masculine nouns work as before: сахар → с сахар**ом**, лимон → с лимон**ом**, сыр → с сыр**ом**. This is how you order things in a café.

examples.english: Tea with sugar, please.
I love coffee with milk.
Bread with cheese is very tasty.

reading.title: В кафе
reading.text: — Что вы хотите? — Чай с лимоном и хлеб с сыром, пожалуйста. — Очень вкусно!
reading.translation: — What would you like? — Tea with lemon and bread with cheese, please. — Very tasty!

days.speakingPrompt: Order two things in a café using "...пожалуйста". For example: "Чай с молоком, пожалуйста" and "Хлеб с сыром, пожалуйста".

days.title: Чем ты пишешь? — Tools and means

vocab.exampleEn: The child draws with a pencil.
I cut bread with a knife.
I eat soup with a spoon.

grammar.title: Instrumental for the tool (no preposition!)
grammar.explanation: To say what tool or instrument you use, put the noun in the **instrumental case with NO preposition**. It answers the question **чем?** ("with what / by what means?"). Masculine: нож → нож**о́м**, карандаш → карандаш**о́м**. Feminine: ручка → ручк**ой**, ложка → ложк**ой**. Compare: **с другом** (with a person, uses с) versus **ручкой** (with a pen, no с).

examples.english: I write with a pen.
He draws with a pencil.
We eat soup with a spoon.

reading.title: В школе
reading.text: Брат пишет ручкой, а сестра рисует карандашом. Мама режет хлеб ножом.
reading.translation: My brother writes with a pen, and my sister draws with a pencil. Mum cuts bread with a knife.

days.speakingPrompt: Say what you use for three actions, with no preposition. For example: "Я пишу ручкой", "Я ем ложкой", "Я режу ножом".

days.title: Кто ты по профессии? — Professions

vocab.exampleEn: My brother works as an engineer.
She works as a shop assistant.
He wants to be a cook.

grammar.title: Profession with быть and работать (instrumental)
grammar.explanation: In the **present tense** we say a profession with the nominative and no verb: **Мой папа учитель** (My dad is a teacher). But after the verbs **работать** ("to work as") and **быть** ("to be"), the profession goes into the **instrumental**: работать врач**о́м**, быть повар**ом**, работать инженер**ом**. Soft nouns like учитель take **-ем**: быть учител**ем**.

examples.english: My mum is a doctor.
My brother works as an engineer.
I want to be a cook.

reading.title: Моя семья
reading.text: Мама — врач, а папа работает учителем. Я хочу быть поваром. Это хорошая профессия.
reading.translation: Mum is a doctor, and dad works as a teacher. I want to be a cook. It is a good profession.

days.speakingPrompt: Say what two people you know do, using both patterns: "Мама — врач" (no verb) and "Папа работает учителем" (with работать + instrumental).

days.title: С удовольствием — putting "with whom & how" together

vocab.exampleEn: I am talking with a colleague.
I am chatting with a friend.

grammar.title: All three uses of the instrumental together
grammar.explanation: This week's instrumental has three jobs. **(1) с + person** = with someone: разговаривать **с коллегой**. **(2) tool, no preposition** = by means of: писать **ручкой**. **(3) profession** after работать/быть: работать **инженером**. There is also a handy fixed phrase: **с удовольствием** = "with pleasure / gladly".

examples.english: I am chatting with the boss.
She works as a shop assistant.
I help with pleasure.

reading.title: На работе
reading.text: Я работаю инженером. Я работаю с компьютером и разговариваю с коллегой. Я помогаю с удовольствием.
reading.translation: I work as an engineer. I work with a computer and chat with a colleague. I help with pleasure.

days.speakingPrompt: Make three sentences mixing the uses: one with a person (с коллегой), one with a tool (компьютером), and one profession (работаю...). End one sentence with "с удовольствием".

days.title: Review: With whom & how

grammar.title: Instrumental case recap
grammar.explanation: Three uses this week: **(1)** **с + instrumental** for accompaniment — с друг**ом**, с сестр**ой**, с молок**ом**. **(2)** **instrumental alone** for a tool/means — ручк**ой**, нож**о́й**. **(3)** **instrumental after работать/быть** for professions — работать врач**о́м**, быть учител**ем**. Endings: masc. **-ом/-ем**, fem. **-ой/-ей**, neuter **-ом**. Remember: people get **с**, tools do not.

examples.english: I am walking with a friend.
I eat soup with a spoon.
Dad works as a cook.

reading.title: Кто ты и с кем?
reading.text: — Кто ты по профессии? — Я врач. Я работаю с коллегой. — А дома? — Дома я живу с мамой и братом. Мы пьём чай с лимоном.
reading.translation: — What is your profession? — I am a doctor. I work with a colleague. — And at home? — At home I live with my mum and brother. We drink tea with lemon.

days.speakingPrompt: Answer out loud: "Кто ты по профессии?" and "С кем ты живёшь?". Then say one thing you do with a tool (e.g. "Я пишу ручкой") and use the phrase "с удовольствием" once.
